skill.name: azure-compute
skill.description: Recommends Azure virtual machine sizes, VM Scale Sets, and pricing options using public Microsoft documentation and the Azure Retail Prices API.
skill.summary: Choose Azure VM and VMSS configurations for workload, scale, and budget requirements.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are Markdown examples and public Microsoft documentation links, not executable code or malicious behavior. The remaining risk is low because the skill intentionally asks the assistant to fetch public Azure documentation and pricing data.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Command Examples
low_findings.description: The external-command alerts match fenced Markdown examples for web_fetch, curl, and Invoke-RestMethod. They guide users to query public Microsoft documentation and the Azure Retail Prices API; they are not executable skill code. Verdict: false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside Markdown code fences or instructional text. No script file, executable hook, or hidden command runner is present in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Public Microsoft Network References
low_findings.description: The network alerts identify hardcoded URLs for learn.microsoft.com, azure.microsoft.com, and prices.azure.com. These endpoints support documentation verification and pricing lookup, and no credential or user data exfiltration pattern was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All reviewed URLs point to Microsoft-owned public documentation or pricing services. The files do not include secret collection, arbitrary endpoint construction, or non-Microsoft destinations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts occur on metadata, VM family descriptions, pricing table text, or documentation links. No cryptographic operation, cipher choice, hashing function, or random-number generation was found at the cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Azure VM products, API fields, or VMSS orchestration concepts. They contain no code that performs encryption, hashing, signing, or randomness.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The system-reconnaissance alerts match benign documentation about filtering retail price records and VMSS autoscale cooldowns. No local host inspection, environment probing, filesystem traversal, or process enumeration was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is ordinary cloud guidance and API response field documentation. The reviewed files do not include commands that inspect the user machine or runtime environment.

content.user_title: Choose Azure VM and VMSS Options
content.value_statement: Choosing an Azure VM or VM Scale Set can be slow when workload, pricing, and availability needs are unclear. This skill turns requirements into practical VM, VMSS, and cost recommendations using public Azure references.
actual_capabilities: Maps workload requirements to Azure VM families and candidate SKUs.
Compares single VM and VM Scale Set hosting models.
Uses public Azure pricing API examples for pay-as-you-go and reservation estimates.
Explains VMSS orchestration, autoscale, availability, and load balancing trade-offs.
Prompts verification against current Microsoft Learn documentation before final recommendations.
limitations: Does not deploy, modify, or inspect Azure resources.
Requires live documentation checks for the most current Azure SKU details.
Pricing examples may need region, OS, and licensing details from the user.
Does not replace architecture review for regulated or mission-critical workloads.

use_cases.title: Plan a Web Tier
use_cases.description: Select a balanced VM or VMSS design for a stateless web application with autoscale needs.

use_cases.title: Estimate Compute Cost
use_cases.description: Compare VM options by hourly and monthly cost before creating an Azure estimate.

use_cases.title: Match Specialized Workloads
use_cases.description: Identify candidate families for GPU, memory-heavy, storage-heavy, confidential, or HPC workloads.

prompt_templates.title: Recommend a Basic VM
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend an Azure VM for a small Linux web application in eastus. Prioritize low cost and explain the trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Compare VM Families
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare D-series, B-series, and F-series Azure VMs for a CI workload with variable CPU demand and a limited budget.

prompt_templates.title: Design a VMSS Option
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Azure VM Scale Set option for a stateless API. Include orchestration mode, autoscale range, load balancer type, and cost considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Costed Shortlist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a shortlist of three Azure compute options for a memory-heavy database workload. Verify current docs, estimate pricing, and explain when each option wins.

output_examples.input: I need a low-cost VM for a development web server in East US.
output_examples.output: A concise recommendation with one burstable option, one balanced option, estimated hourly cost, and a note about CPU credit limits.

output_examples.input: Should my stateless API use one VM or a VM Scale Set?
output_examples.output: A VM versus VMSS comparison that covers autoscale, high availability, load balancing, operational complexity, and cost range.

output_examples.input: Find Azure options for GPU inference with predictable demand.
output_examples.output: A shortlist of GPU families, likely SKU candidates, pricing lookup guidance, and reservations or savings plan considerations.

best_practices: Provide workload type, region, operating system, scale target, and budget priority before asking for recommendations.
Verify candidate VM family documentation before making a final decision.
Estimate VMSS costs at minimum, expected, and maximum instance counts.
anti_patterns: Using VMSS for a single long-lived server with unique configuration needs.
Choosing the cheapest VM without checking CPU credits, memory pressure, or disk throughput.
Treating reference prices as final bills without accounting for storage, networking, licensing, and discounts.

faq.question: Does this skill need an Azure subscription?
faq.answer: No. It uses public Microsoft documentation and the unauthenticated Azure Retail Prices API.

faq.question: Can it deploy a VM or VM Scale Set?
faq.answer: No. It provides recommendations only. Use a deployment-focused skill or Azure tooling for provisioning.

faq.question: Does it support VMSS recommendations?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare single VMs with VMSS and explain orchestration, autoscale, and load balancing choices.

faq.question: How current are the recommendations?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the assistant to verify final details with current Microsoft Learn documentation.

faq.question: Can it estimate Windows and Linux prices?
faq.answer: Yes, when the user provides operating system and region details. Prices can differ by OS and license terms.

faq.question: Is this a cost optimization tool for running workloads?
faq.answer: No. It helps choose compute options before deployment, not analyze existing Azure spend.
